data.body:
RESOLVED on HEAD by commit 2b13e6ae6 ('tests: 85c3: de-flake three merge-gating tests — harness race, wall-clock perf, over-broad sleep patch').

Root cause confirmed: the flaky assertion was the wall-clock doubling-ratio 't(2N)/t(N) < 3.0' in tests/scripts/graph/test_graph_perf.py::test_graph_build_scales_linearly (introduced by cefd14be3 as a relative metric, but still flaked at ~3.64 on loaded macOS runners because t(1000)≈0.9s is too small/noisy for a stable ratio).

Remediation (AC-satisfying): 2b13e6ae6 DELETED test_graph_build_scales_linearly and the sibling test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_relative (130 LOC of wall-clock timing removed), and the O(n) build-scan invariant is now enforced LOAD-INDEPENDENTLY by operation-count structural tests in the same file:
  - test_build_dep_graph_single_batch_scan (reduce_all_tickets called exactly once)
  - test_find_direct_blockers_no_per_ticket_scan (_reduce_ticket called 0 times)
  - test_compute_dep_graph_children_use_preloaded_state (_reduce_ticket called 0 times)
A reintroduced O(n^2) per-ticket rescan breaks these call-count assertions deterministically — this is exactly the 'operation-count proxy instead of wall-clock' option named in AC.

AC verification:
- [x] scaling check no longer flakes on macOS CI — the wall-clock test is gone; zero timing assertions remain (grep clean).
- [x] still catches a genuine super-linear regression — the 3 structural call-count tests fail on any per-ticket rescan regression.
- [x] distinct class from the git-add store-write flake (edf7/73d8).

Regression coverage runs in the gerrit-verify Verified gate: the 3 structural tests are @pytest.mark.unit under tests/scripts/graph/, collected by gerrit-verify's 'Run the default suite' step (pytest -m 'not integration and not external', testpaths=[tests]). Verified 5 passed locally on HEAD.
